notes: Strong implementation overall. Security: Good use of timingSafeEqual for HMAC, security headers, rate limiting, body size limits, and no internal error leakage. However, the HMAC algorithm is parsed from user input (attacker can downgrade from sha256 to md5), x-forwarded-for is trusted without proxy validation (spoofable IPs weaken rate limiting), CORS defaults to '*', and prototype pollution check is shallow (top-level only). Error handling: Comprehensive custom error classes, global try/catch, unhandledRejection/uncaughtException handlers, headersSent guard, and good logging context. Missing request-level timeout means a hanging handler blocks indefinitely. Best practices: Clean structure, private fields, structured logging, requestId tracing, proper keepAliveTimeout/headersTimeout. PORT is listed in REQUIRED_ENV but has a fallback default (contradiction/dead code). No module exports or test surface -- monolithic file is not unit-testable. No slow-loris protection on body reading. Robustness: BoundedTTLMap with periodic pruning prevents memory leaks, graceful shutdown with force timeout is well done, partial failure tracking across multiple handlers is thoughtful. However, in-memory-only idempotency store loses all state on restart, 'processing' status has no crash recovery (stuck until TTL), and the check-then-set idempotency logic is not atomic (race condition on concurrent duplicate events).
